description:
You are a security engineer who has just finished a large-scale credential rotation.  
A raw, unsorted list of every username that had its password rotated was dumped to

/home/user/credentials/rotations.log

(one username per line, no extra spaces).

Goal: generate a concise frequency report that tells, for every unique username, how many times it appears in the rotation log.

1. Read /home/user/credentials/rotations.log  
2. Using only standard GNU coreutils commands (e.g. sort, uniq, pipes, output-redirection), create

/home/user/credentials/rotation_frequency.txt

3. rotation_frequency.txt must satisfy **all** of the following rules so that automated tests pass:

   • One line per unique username.  
   • Each line’s format is:
     username<single space>count<newline>

     (Exactly one ASCII space between the username and the decimal count; no leading zeros, no extra whitespace.)  
   • Lines are ordered:
     a. Primary key – descending numeric count (highest counts first)  
     b. Secondary key – ascending lexicographical order of the username when counts tie.  

4. Do not create or modify any other files.

Hint: the whole job can be done with a maximum of 2 – 3 shell commands glued together by pipes/redirection.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts
-------------------------------------
Directory: /home/user/credentials  (already exists and is writable)
File: /home/user/credentials/rotations.log
Exact contents (each entry terminated by a UNIX newline):

alice
bob
charlie
alice
alice
bob
dave
eve
bob
frank

No trailing blank lines.

Expected artefact after successful completion
---------------------------------------------
File: /home/user/credentials/rotation_frequency.txt
Exact required contents (each line terminated by a UNIX newline, no trailing blank line):

alice 3
bob 3
charlie 1
dave 1
eve 1
frank 1
name: task_000000_3b8ceb80
